This is a full upgrade to how Gemini creates images, better knowledge, better text, better control, all inside the tool you already use. Today, I'm breaking down every single new feature, showing you exactly how to use them, and giving you real examples you can steal right now. Let's go. Right, five new features just dropped inside Gemini's image model, and each one matters. Here they are. Improved real world knowledge, advanced text rendering, image generation templates, aspect ratio control, and character preservation. I'm going to walk [music] you through every single one, and I'm going to show you how to actually use them with real examples so you can see what this looks like in the real world. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every comment, so make sure you comment below. Improved real world knowledge. So before this update, Gemini's image model had a knowledge problem. You'd ask it to generate something with real context, like a modern office, a current brand style, a realistic business setting, and it would miss. It felt outdated, generic, off. Now with Nano Banana 2, the model has been trained on better, more current data. That means when you ask it to create something that reflects the real world, it actually gets it right. Here's a practical example. Let's say you want to create a hero image for a new AI profit boardroom landing page. You want it to look modern, premium, and reflect how real AI automation businesses actually look. You'd prompt it like this. Create a professional hero image for an AI automation community called the AI profit boardroom. Show a modern entrepreneur at a clean workspace using AI tools. Reflect a current premium brand feel with deep navy and gold tones. Because the model now has stronger real world knowledge, it actually understands what a premium modern AI brand should look like, not some clip art from 2015. Something that looks real that matters for landing pages, ads, social content, thumbnails, anything visual. Better knowledge means better outputs. Simple. And and look, if you want to go deeper on tools like this and see exactly how they're being used to automate real businesses, check out the AI Profit Boardroom. It is the best place to scale your business, get more customers, and save hundreds of hours using AI automation. Link is in the comment and description. Advanced text rendering. This one is huge. And if you've ever used AI image tools before, you know exactly why. One of the biggest problems with AI image generation has always been text inside images. You ask the model to put words inside an image and it comes out garbled. Wrong letters, broken words, unreadable garbage. Every AI image tool has struggled with this for years. Nano Banana 2 makes a serious leap forward here. You can now generate images with real readable text inside them. Logos, banners, thumbnails, ads with text that actually looks right. Here's a real example. Let's say you want a social media graphic promoting the AI profit boardroom. You want it to say, "Join 45,000 members inside AI Profit Boardroom." Before this update, that text would come out broken, misspelled, useless. Now you prompt it like this. Create a bold clean social media graphic with the text. Join 38,000 members AI profit boardroom in large white font on a deep navy background with gold accents professional modern style. And Gemini can now actually produce that image with the text intact, readable, and welldesigned. That means less back and forth, less jumping between tools, less time wasted fixing AI mistakes manually. For anyone creating content at scale, this alone is worth paying attention to. Image generation templates. Here's where it gets really practical. Gemini has now introduced image generation templates. Instead of starting from scratch every time, you now have structured starting points. Templates that guide the style, layout, and format of your image before you even write a single word. Think of it like Canva templates, but for AI generated images. And this solves one of the hardest problems with AI visuals, consistency. Because right now, if you generate 10 images for your brand without a template, they all look slightly different. Different style, different feel, different vibe. It looks messy. Templates fix that. For the AI profit boardroom, this means you can lock in a visual style. Deep navy, gold accents, clean text, professional layout, and then every time you generate a new image, you start from that template and just change the message. That's how real brands build recognition. Consistent visuals every time. And now AI can actually match that at scale without a designer. This is massive for anyone managing content, running ads, or building a brand. Aspect ratio control. This one sounds small, but trust me, it matters. Gemini now gives you direct control over the aspect ratio of your generated images. Before this update, you get whatever size the model decided to give you, and then you'd have to go resize it yourself in another tool. wasted steps, wasted time. Now you can tell Gemini exactly what dimensions you need before it generates the image. Square for Instagram, wide for YouTube thumbnails, vertical for stories and Tik Tok, portrait for Pinterest. You pick it, it delivers it. Here's how this works in practice. Let's say you're running a full content push for the AI profit boardroom across multiple platforms. You need one image concept, but in three different formats. You'd prompt it like this. Generate three versions of a promotional image for the AI profit boardroom. One in 1.1 square format for Instagram. One in 16.9 landscape for YouTube. One in 916 portrait for Instagram stories. Deep navy background. Gold accents. Clean modern style. One prompt. Three formats. All consistent, all ready to use. That is a real workflow improvement. Especially if you're creating content regularly or running any kind of paid traffic. Character preservation. Okay, this is the one I said to stick around for, and here's why. Character preservation means that Gemini can now keep a specific character or person consistent across multiple images. In plain English, you create a character once and then every image you generate using that character looks like the same person, same face, same features, same style across every single image. This has been one of the hardest problems in AI image generation. You create a person in one image, try to generate another image with the same person, and they look completely different. Different face, different hair, totally different human. That makes it nearly impossible to build any kind of consistent brand character, mascot, or AI persona. Now, that's solved. Think about what this means for content creators, coaches, course creators, and business owners. Let's say you want to create a branded AI avatar for the AI profit boardroom. A digital character that represents the brand across all your content, your YouTube thumbnails, your social post, your ad creatives. Before character preservation, every image of that avatar looked different, unusable for branding. Now you create the character once and Gemini keeps it consistent every time you generate a new image. For anyone building a personal brand or a business brand using AI, this is a gamecher. This is also huge for creating course materials, lead magnets, presentation slides, anything where you want the same visual character showing up repeatedly without hiring a designer or a photographer. Putting it all together. So, let's recap what just dropped inside Gemini with Nano Banana 2. Real world knowledge so your images actually look current and credible. Advanced text rendering so words inside images are actually readable. templates so your brand stays consistent without starting from scratch. Aspect ratio control so you stop resizing images manually and character preservation so you can finally build a consistent AI persona across all your content. Five features all inside the Gemini app available now. And look, none of this requires any design skills. No Photoshop, no expensive tools, no team. Just Gemini, a good prompt and a clear idea of what you want. That's the power of where AI is right now, and it's only getting better.
